picks.game: Boston Red Sox @ New York Mets
picks.rationale: The Red Sox are on an impressive eight-game winning streak, having swept their last two series on the road and aiming for a third. Rookie left-hander Payton Tolle, Boston's scheduled starter, is coming off a dominant outing where he pitched six scoreless innings. In contrast, the Mets' offense has been anemic, scoring only two runs in the first two games of this series and struggling with runners in scoring position. Key Mets player Bo Bichette is also dealing with an ankle injury, further hindering their offensive capabilities.

The Chicago White Sox are coming off a dominant 14-1 victory over the Athletics just two days ago. The Athletics are in poor form, having lost four of their last five games, and are missing key first baseman Nick Kurtz due to a thumb injury. This recent head-to-head blowout, coupled with the Athletics' struggles and injury, gives the White Sox a significant advantage to win by more than one run.

The St. Louis Cardinals have demonstrated recent superiority over the Atlanta Braves, winning their last two head-to-head matchups by identical 2-1 scores. The Cardinals also hold a 4-1 record in their last five games against the Braves. Atlanta is struggling, having lost four of their last five overall games, and is without outfielder Mike Yastrzemski due to elbow inflammation.

The Arizona Diamondbacks are currently on a three-game winning streak, with all three victories coming against the Los Angeles Dodgers in this series. The Dodgers, despite their strong overall record, are in a significant slump, having lost their last three games to the Diamondbacks and four of their last five overall. While the Diamondbacks are missing Zac Gallen, their recent performance against the Dodgers, who are also missing Will Smith, suggests they can keep this game close or even win outright.

Both the Guardians and Marlins offenses have shown recent power, with Miami hitting 19 home runs and slugging .521 in their last 10 games, and Cleveland hitting 14 home runs with a .406 slugging percentage in the same span. The Marlins' pitching staff has a 4.45 ERA over their last 10 outings, while the Guardians' staff holds a 3.43 ERA, but both have allowed a significant number of home runs. With two offenses in good form and pitching that has shown vulnerability, especially from the Marlins, this game has a high probability of exceeding the 9.5 total.
